postData.contentHtml:
The recent funeral for Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei became a potent display of anti-American sentiment, as a performer publicly demanded the death of U.S. President Donald Trump. The call, made during solemn ceremonies for the leader, who was reportedly killed in an airstrike, resonated with thousands of mourners in Tehran.

Reports from the funeral highlighted widespread chants of "Death to America!" and the visible display of "#KillTrump" banners among the gathered crowds. This fervent demonstration of animosity occurred during a sensitive period, as peace talks between the United States and Iran had been temporarily paused. The timing of the funeral, coinciding with the 250th anniversary of the U.S., added another layer of symbolism to the anti-American rhetoric.

The event underscores the complex and often volatile relationship between the two nations, even as diplomatic channels attempt to navigate a path towards de-escalation. While the simulated social sentiment around the event was noted as "mostly positive, with some debate," this likely reflects internal support within Iran for such expressions, contrasting sharply with international reactions. The powerful imagery and explicit calls for vengeance against the U.S. and Israel signal a continued hardline stance from elements within the Iranian populace, particularly in the wake of Khamenei's death.
postData.title: "Death to America" Chants and #KillTrump Banners Erupt at Iranian Supreme Leader's Funeral
postData.summary: Amidst the funeral ceremonies for Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, a performer publicly called for the death of U.S. President Trump. This provocative act was amplified by large crowds chanting "Death to America" and displaying "#KillTrump" banners, underscoring deep anti-American sentiment during a critical pause in U.S.-Iran peace talks.

sources.title: Performer calls for Trump's death at funeral for Iran's late supreme leader
Iran begins dayslong funeral for Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed in war
'Death to America': Crowds at Khamenei funeral call for revenge against US-Israel and wave '#KillTrump' banner
